methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: User sends tokenIn to Pair
Pair computes amountIn from balance delta
Fee = amountIn * factory.getFee / 10000
_update0/_update1 called with fee amount
Referral fee deducted and sent to dibs
Staking fee tracked via PairFees.processStakingFees
Remaining amount transferred to PairFees contract
LP fee index updated for LP holders
data_flows.trust_changes: PairFactory fee manager controls all fee percentages; no upper bound on referral fee
validation_points: K invariant check after fee extraction
exposure_risks: Fee distribution ratios configurable by feeManager
manipulation_risks: feeManager can set MAX_REFERRAL_FEE to 10000 (100%), diverting all fees to referral
Rounding down in index calculation causes dust loss for LP holders
Fee-on-transfer tokens cause incorrect amountIn calculation

path: User calls swapExactTokensForTokens / swapExactTokensForTokensSimple
getAmountsOut computes expected outputs from routes[].pair
Tokens transferred from user to first pair (or router for CL)
_swap iterates routes, using routes[i].receiver as destination
Final output sent to routes[last].receiver
transformations: amountIn -> getAmountsOut via pair.getAmountOut or quoterV2
CL swaps: amounts set by ISwapRouter with amountOutMinimum=0
sinks: Final recipient from route struct (NOT _to parameter)
data_flows.trust_changes: User-controlled route.receiver determines token destination; _to parameter in _swap is dead code
exposure_risks: CL swaps use amountOutMinimum=0 allowing sandwich attacks within individual hops
manipulation_risks: swapExactTokensForTokensSimple never sets routes[0].pair or routes[0].receiver, making function completely broken
UNSAFE_swapExactTokensForTokens allows arbitrary amounts with no price verification

path: GenesisPool calls depositsForGenesis with tokenOwner, timestamp, totalAmount
updateReward(address(0)) updates global state but NOT user state
TOKEN transferred from genesisPool to gauge
_totalSupply increased but _balances[tokenOwner] NOT increased
Balance tracked via genesisPool.balanceOf()
data_flows.trust_changes: Genesis depositor's userRewardPerTokenPaid never set at deposit time
exposure_risks: User reward checkpoint not updated at deposit time
manipulation_risks: If rewards were already distributing before genesis deposit, depositor retroactively earns past rewards because userRewardPerTokenPaid[user] remains 0

path: Distribution calls notifyRewardAmount with reward amount
rewardRate calculated from reward / DURATION
Users accrue rewards via rewardPerToken() over time
Users call getReward() to claim accumulated rewards
data_flows.trust_changes: DISTRIBUTION address fully controls reward amounts
manipulation_risks: Genesis depositors can steal rewards by not having userRewardPerTokenPaid set at deposit time
_balanceOf includes genesisPool balance which can be manipulated by genesis pool contract

path: Router transfers tokens to Pair
Pair.mint calculates liquidity from deposited amounts
_updateFor called for recipient to checkpoint fees
LP tokens minted to recipient
reserves updated via _update
transformations: sqrt(amount0 * amount1) - MINIMUM_LIQUIDITY for first mint
min(amount0 * totalSupply / reserve0, amount1 * totalSupply / reserve1) for subsequent mints
data_flows.trust_changes: No access control on mint/burn (relies on router for safety checks)
exposure_risks: First depositor can manipulate initial price with donation attack
manipulation_risks: Front-running first deposit to manipulate price ratio

path: Owner calls setDistributionRate with amount
rewardPerSecond calculated
On gauge deposit/withdraw, onReward called
updatePool recalculates accRewardPerShare using GAUGE TOKEN balance
Pending reward calculated and transferred to user
transformations: time * rewardPerSecond * ACC_TOKEN_PRECISION / lpSupply -> accRewardPerShare increment
exposure_risks: lpSupply uses balanceOf(GAUGE) which may differ from gauge._totalSupply
manipulation_risks: Potential underflow in pending calculation if rewardDebt exceeds computed value due to rounding
lpSupply mismatch if tokens sent directly to gauge without deposit()

core_rules: K invariant: x*y >= k (volatile) or x³y + xy³ >= k (stable) must hold after every swap
Fees are extracted before K invariant check
LP tokens represent pro-rata share of pool reserves
Gauge rewards distributed proportional to staked LP balance over time
Referral fees deducted before staking and LP fee calculations
economic_incentives: LP holders earn swap fees proportional to their LP share
Gauge stakers earn emission rewards proportional to their staked LP
Voters direct emissions to gauges, earning bribes in return
Referral system incentivizes bringing trade volume
Staking NFT fee creates additional revenue for protocol stakers
governance_mechanisms: feeManager controls all fee parameters with no timelock
Owner can pause all swaps via PairFactory.isPaused
EmergencyCouncil can activate emergency mode on gauges

edge_cases.scenario: GaugeV2.depositsForGenesis called after notifyRewardAmount has been distributing rewards
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Genesis depositor should only earn rewards from the time of their deposit
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Genesis depositor earns rewards retroactively from start because updateReward(address(0)) does not set userRewardPerTokenPaid for the depositor
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Genesis depositor can claim rewards they were not entitled to, stealing from other stakers. The theft amount equals balanceOf(depositor) * rewardPerTokenStored / 1e18 at time of first interaction after deposit.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Simple single-hop swap should execute successfully
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Function is completely broken: routes[0].pair and routes[0].receiver default to address(0). For non-concentrated: getAmountsOut returns 0 because isPair(address(0)) is false. For concentrated: quoter call fails with invalid deployer. Function always reverts.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Not exploitable per se, but renders a core protocol function non-functional. Users lose gas fees on failed transactions.

edge_cases.scenario: RouterV2._swap called with _to parameter set to user address
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Final swap output should be sent to _to address
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _to parameter is completely ignored; output goes to routes[last].receiver instead. For swapExactTokensForETH, if last route receiver is not address(this), WETH won't arrive at router and withdraw fails.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Tokens sent to wrong address if routes[i].receiver is not correctly set. In swapExactTokensForETH, funds could be stuck if receiver mismatch occurs.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should be bounded to a reasonable maximum
edge_cases.actual_behavior: No upper bound check. Setting to 10000 causes 100% of swap fees to go to referral system, leaving 0 for LPs and stakers.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Compromised or malicious feeManager can divert all swap fees from LP holders and stakers to the referral system

edge_cases.scenario: RouterV2._swapSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens with stable and volatile pools for same pair
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should use the pair specified in the route for amount calculation
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Uses router's getAmountOut which selects the best pool (stable or volatile), not necessarily the one specified in the route. amountOutput may be calculated for wrong pool.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If the best pool returns higher amountOut than the actual pair, swap will fail (K invariant). If lower, user receives less than possible. Can cause unexpected reverts or suboptimal swaps.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should return required input amounts for each hop
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Infinite loop: uint i decrements from routes.length-1 to 0, then underflows to type(uint).max, looping forever until out-of-gas.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Function is non-functional, always reverts. Any integrator relying on this function will have broken functionality.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should correctly compute required input for desired output
edge_cases.actual_behavior: y_1 = reserveOut + output (line adds output instead of subtracting). Also uses decimalsOut instead of decimalsIn for final conversion. Returns incorrect amountIn values.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Returns wrong values for integrators. If used for pricing, could lead to unfavorable trades. View-only so no direct fund loss from this contract.

edge_cases.scenario: Pair.current called when only one observation exists
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should return a valid TWAP price
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If block.timestamp == lastObservation.timestamp, accesses observations[length-2] which underflows for single observation, causing revert.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Oracle can be made unreliable by querying immediately after pair creation. View function so no direct fund loss.

edge_cases.scenario: Pair.sample called with points * window > observations.length
edge_cases.actual_behavior: uint underflow in: i = length - (points * window), causing extremely large i and out-of-bounds array access revert
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Oracle queries revert, potentially affecting dependent protocols

edge_cases.actual_behavior: require(_int >= address(0)) always passes since all addresses >= address(0). Internal bribe can be set to address(0), breaking fee distribution.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Owner (or admin through factory) can set internal_bribe to address(0), causing claimFees to fail when trying to approve/notifyRewardAmount on zero address

edge_cases.scenario: CL swap via RouterV2._swap with concentrated=true
edge_cases.actual_behavior: amountOutMinimum is hardcoded to 0 in ExactInputSingleParams, providing zero slippage protection per hop for concentrated liquidity swaps
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Sandwich attacks on individual CL swap hops. Overall swap has amountOutMin check, but intermediate hops can be manipulated.

edge_cases.scenario: WAVAX.withdraw called by a contract with expensive receive function
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Uses payable(msg.sender).transfer(wad) which forwards only 2300 gas, insufficient for contracts with non-trivial receive/fallback logic
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Smart contract wallets or multisigs may be unable to withdraw WAVAX

edge_cases.actual_behavior: Function body is entirely commented out, returns 0 for amountOut silently
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Integrators calling this function would receive 0 tokens without error, potentially losing their input if combined with other operations

edge_cases.scenario: RouterV2 owner functions called after key compromise
edge_cases.actual_behavior: No owner transfer function exists. Owner set in constructor is permanent.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If owner key is compromised, attacker permanently controls swapRouter, algebraFactory, quoterV2, and algebraPoolAPIStorage settings

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Fees calculated on actual received amount
edge_cases.actual_behavior: amountIn calculated from balance delta (correct), but fee transferred via _safeTransfer may deliver less to PairFees than expected with FoT tokens, causing index tracking mismatch
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Fee accounting becomes incorrect for FoT tokens; LP holders may be unable to claim all fees

assumption_violations: TradeHelper.getAmountsIn assumes uint can safely decrement past 0 - it cannot, causing infinite loop
swapExactTokensForTokensSimple assumes route struct fields are initialized - pair and receiver are not
_swap assumes _to parameter is used - it is dead code, only routes[i].receiver matters
depositsForGenesis assumes rewards haven't started accumulating - no guarantee of this
getAmountInStable assumes output increases reserve - it should decrease it
GaugeExtraRewarder assumes user.amount * accRewardPerShare >= user.rewardDebt - rounding can violate this
setInternalBribe validation assumes address comparison works for zero check - >= address(0) is always true
game_theory_risks: Genesis depositors can steal accumulated rewards from existing gauge stakers if rewards were already being distributed
Fee manager can extract all swap fees via unbounded referral fee setting
UNSAFE_swapExactTokensForTokens allows arbitrary amount specification enabling sandwich attacks
Oracle manipulation possible through observations array bounds issues affecting TWAP calculations
integration_risks: Algebra CL integration uses amountOutMinimum=0 per hop, no per-hop slippage protection
algebraPoolAPIStorage.pairToDeployer must return valid deployer for CL swaps to work
PairFees contract must always have sufficient balance for both LP claims and staking fee withdrawals
External gaugeRewarder callback could revert, blocking deposits/withdrawals
IGenesisPool.balanceOf must be consistent with gauge accounting
calculation_errors: TradeHelper.getAmountInStable: y_1 = reserveOut + output should be reserveOut - output
TradeHelper.getAmountInStable: uses decimalsOut for amountIn conversion instead of decimalsIn
Pair._update0/_update1: ratio = amount * 1e18 / totalSupply rounds down, causing dust fee loss over time
GaugeExtraRewarder._pendingReward: potential underflow revert in (user.amount * accRewardPerShare / ACC_TOKEN_PRECISION) - user.rewardDebt

token_semantics.notes: ERC20 LP token with custom permit implementation. DOMAIN_SEPARATOR recomputed on each permit call. _updateFor called on all balance changes for fee tracking.
Standard wrapped native token. Uses .transfer (2300 gas limit) for withdrawals which may fail for complex receivers.
Emission reward token distributed through GaugeV2. Used with SafeERC20 in gauge contracts.

Arbitrary ERC20 tokens in pairs. Fee-on-transfer tokens cause accounting issues in Pair._update0/_update1 and PairFees. Router has separate FoT-supporting swap functions but they use wrong getAmountOut.

invariants.name: k_invariant_volatile
invariants.rule: After swap: reserve0_new * reserve1_new >= reserve0_old * reserve1_old

invariants.name: lp_supply_conservation
invariants.rule: totalSupply == sum of all balanceOf values

invariants.name: fee_index_monotonicity
invariants.rule: index0 and index1 should only increase over time

invariants.name: gauge_supply_balance
invariants.rule: _totalSupply should equal TOKEN.balanceOf(gauge) minus any unrealized reward token balance overlap

invariants.name: pairfees_balance_coverage
invariants.rule: token.balanceOf(PairFees) >= toStake0 + sum(unclaimed LP fees for token0) for each token
